fact: The Iroquois Theatre fire occurred on December 30th, 1903
source: Tinder Box: The Iroquois Theatre Disaster 1903, Anthony P. Hatch (2003)

fact: The theatre held approximately 1,900 seats and the matinee audience was largely women and children
source: Chicago Tribune historical coverage; also Hatch, Tinder Box (2003)

fact: A stage light ignited drapery/curtain material above the stage
source: National Fire Protection Association (NFPA) case study; also Hatch, Tinder Box (2003)

fact: The asbestos safety curtain jammed partway down and failed to seal the proscenium opening
source: Tinder Box: The Iroquois Theatre Disaster 1903, Anthony P. Hatch (2003)

fact: 602 people died in the fire, making it one of the deadliest single-building fires in U.S. history
source: Widely documented; the commonly cited toll is 602. See NFPA records and Hatch, Tinder Box (2003)

fact: The theatre had been advertised and certified as fireproof and had recently passed safety inspections
source: Tinder Box: The Iroquois Theatre Disaster 1903, Anthony P. Hatch (2003); Chicago Historical Society records
